briefdescription.para: The number of orbital blocks per particle type (length ntypes).
The maximal capacity of each orbital block.
The number of particles of each class in total (length ntypes, used to determine Aufbau occupations).
The Fock builder used to evaluate energies and Fock matrices.

detaileddescription.para: Optional batched Fock builder; if unset, evaluate_batch_ loops over fock_builder_ instead.

briefdescription.para: Descriptions of the blocks.

memberdef.definition: std::function<void(const std::map<std::string,std::any> & data)> OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::callback_function_
memberdef.name: callback_function_

briefdescription.para: Callback function.

memberdef.definition: std::function<bool(const std::map<std::string,std::any> & data)> OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::callback_convergence_function_
memberdef.name: callback_convergence_function_

briefdescription.para: Callback function to allow calling program to judge convergence (trumps convergence_threshold_).

detaileddescription.para: (Optional) fixed number of particles in each symmetry, affects the way occupations are assigned in Aufbau. These are used if the array has the expected size.

briefdescription.para: (Optional) freeze occupations altogether to their previous values
Verbosity level: 0 for silent, higher values for more info.
The number of blocks.
The orbital history used for convergence acceleration.
Orbital energies, updated each iteration from the lowest-energy solution.
Number of Fock matrix evaluations.
Maximum number of iterations.
Start to mix in DIIS at this error threshold (Garza and Scuseria, 2012).
Threshold for pure DIIS (Garza and Scuseria, 2012).
Damping factor for DIIS diagonal (Hamilton and Pulay, 1986).
DIIS restart criterion (Chupin et al, 2021).
Criterion for max error for which to use optimal damping.
History cleanup criterion: keep only those density matrices that satisfy delta ||P0-Pi|| < min_{j>0} ||P0-Pj||.
History length.
Steps with no DIIS energy improvement after which to use ODA. Previously maximum_history_length_/2.
Convergence threshold for orbital gradient.
Norm to use by default: root-mean-square error.
Minimal normalized projection of preconditioned search direction onto gradient.
Threshold for detection of occupied orbitals.

detaileddescription.para: Initial level shift used as the floor in the orbital-rotation preconditioner: d_alpha = -g_alpha / (sigma + max(0, h_alpha)), starting from sigma = initial_level_shift_ on trial 1 of the sigma-line-search. A small floor keeps the well-conditioned DOFs in their near-Newton regime (where h_alpha dominates the denominator) instead of damping them at the constant 1 Eh that the previous default forced; the line search bumps sigma up adaptively when wrong-sign or near-degenerate DOFs cause the trial step to overshoot.

briefdescription.para: Level shift diminution factor.

detaileddescription.para: Energy gap below which orbitals are treated as degenerate when enumerating skeleton density matrices in optimal damping. The default of 0.05 Eh (~1.36 eV) is loose enough to group orbitals that are degenerate by molecular symmetry but split by O(10 mEh) numerical noise during convergence (typical of transition-metal d-shells under PBE + UHF/UKS) and tight enough to leave the well-separated valence levels of main-group molecules alone. Override through optimal_damping_degeneracy_threshold(eps).

briefdescription.para: Norm-squared tolerance for deduplicating skeleton occupations.

detaileddescription.para: Number of orbital-rotation steps to take after each ODA step (when CG is the next state at all ODA accept with integer occupations still skips CG and hands directly to DIIS). The orbital-rotation steps relax the orbital rotations at the ODA-chosen occupations before DIIS gets its turn. Default value 0 means "use the active-rotation count from the most recent ODA call" the number of orbital- rotation pairs that lie inside a degenerate group and have different occupations, summed across particles and blocks (see last_active_rotation_count_). That count is at most one per Krylov dimension of the badly-preconditioned subspace, so the orbital-rotation burst naturally scales with the hard-to-precondition part of the active space; 1 orbital-rotation step is taken as a floor. Set to an explicit positive value to override.

Number of skeleton dimensions of the most recent ODA call (the N_par variable inside optimal_damping_step). Read by the run() state machine to size the orbital-rotation burst when orbital_rotation_steps_after_oda_ is left at its default.
Number of orbital-rotation DOFs in degenerate groups at the iterate that emerged from the most recent ODA call: sum_p sum_b sum_g #pairs(i,j) in g with n_i != n_j

where g runs over the maximal clusters of orbitals within block b of particle p that have orbital energies within optimal_damping_degeneracy_threshold_ of each other. This is the rotation-DOF count that the diagonal Hessian preconditioner handles poorly; preconditioned CG needs at most this many steps to relax the active subspace at fixed occupations (for a quadratic functional, exactly this many; for DFT, generally fewer with the cubic-Hermite line search). Used as the default orbital-rotation burst length when orbital_rotation_steps_after_oda_ is left at zero.

briefdescription.para: Internal holder for computing deltaE.

detaileddescription.para: Polak-Ribière conjugate-gradient state retained between scaled_steepest_descent_step calls so that we can layer CG on top of the preconditioned steepest-descent step. Reset whenever the orbital basis changes globally (e.g. after a successful ODA step) or whenever the rotation degrees of freedom change.

briefdescription.para: Is the block empty?
Get a block of the density matrix for the ihist:th entry.
Get a block of the orbital occupations for the ihist:th entry.
Get a block of the orbital occupations for the ihist:th entry.
Get lowest energy after the given reference index.
Get the energy for the entry.
Get largest index.
Matrix dimensions.
Get a block of the Fock matrix for the ihist:th entry.
Vectorise.
Vectorise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector< Tbase > &vec, size_t nrows, size_t ncols) const
memberdef.name: matricise

memberdef.name: matricise

briefdescription.para: Compute DIIS residual.

memberdef.name: diis_residual

briefdescription.para: Compute DIIS residual.
Form DIIS error vector for ihist:th entry.
Form DIIS error vector for ihist:th entry.
Compute element of DIIS error matrix.
Form DIIS error matrix.
Calculate DIIS weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector< Tbase > &b, const Matrix< Tbase > &A) const
memberdef.name: aediis_weights

briefdescription.para: Calculate ADIIS weights by minimizing quadratic form.
ADIIS linear term: <D_i - D_0 | F_i - F_0>.
EDIIS linear term: list of energies.
ADIIS quadratic term: <D_i - D_n | F_j - F_n>.
EDIIS quadratic term: -0.5*<D_i - D_j | F_i - F_j>.
Calculate ADIIS weights.
Calculate EDIIS weights.

memberdef.name: minimal_error_sampling_algorithm_weights

detaileddescription.para: Minimal Error Sampling Algorithm (MESA), doi:10.14288/1.0372885

briefdescription.para: Compute density change with given weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &old_occ, const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &new_occ) const

briefdescription.para: Computes the difference between orbital occupations.
Perform DIIS extrapolation of Fock matrix.

memberdef.definition: DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::extrapolate_density
memberdef.name: extrapolate_density

briefdescription.para: Perform DIIS extrapolation of density matrix.

memberdef.argsstring: (const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &reference_occupations, const Orbitals< Torb > &C_reference, const Orbitals< Torb > &C_new) const
memberdef.name: determine_maximum_overlap_occupations

briefdescription.para: Compute maximum overlap orbital occupations.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Orbitals< Torb > &lorb, const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &locc, const Orbitals< Torb > &rorb, const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &rocc) const
memberdef.name: density_overlap

briefdescription.para: Compute density overlap between two sets of orbitals and occupations.
Attempt extrapolation with given weights.
See if given Fock matrix reduces the energy.

memberdef.definition: std::pair< Vector< Tbase >, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::solve_polytope_qp_
memberdef.argsstring: (const Matrix< Tbase > &H, const Vector< Tbase > &g, Tbase E_orig, const std::vector< size_t > &particle_off, const std::vector< size_t > &particle_len) const
memberdef.name: solve_polytope_qp_

detaileddescription.para: Minimise ½ lam^T H lam + g^T lam on the product-of-simplices polytope { lam : lam_i >= 0, sum_{i in particle p} lam_i <= 1 } via a primal active-set QP. Working set is the indices of axes pinned at zero plus the particles whose sum-cap is binding; each iteration solves an equality-constrained KKT system for the step from the current iterate, walks the longest feasible step in that direction, and either drops the most-negative Lagrange multiplier or adds the blocking constraint to the working set. Replaces the exponential per-face enumeration that was previously used inside optimal_damping_step (cost 2^(n_p+1)-1 faces per particle, intractable for the npars in the tens that show up when several differently-occupied orbitals straddle the Aufbau Fermi level in a large degenerate group). H is symmetric but not necessarily positive-definite; the algorithm still terminates because of the iteration cap and falls back to lam = 0 if the KKT system is singular.

Optimal damping algorithm step. For each particle type, builds a list of skeleton density matrices corresponding to the extreme integer fillings of every degenerate orbital group at the current Fock matrix; then minimizes the energy on the product of per-particle simplices spanned by (P_reference, P_skel_1, ..., P_skel_n) using analytic-gradient cubic line searches along axes and pairwise vertex-vertex diagonals. Returns true if an entry strictly below the reference was added to the orbital history.

briefdescription.para: Clean up history from incorrect occupations.
Form list of rotation angles.
Formulate the orbital gradient vector.
Formulate the diagonal orbital Hessian.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector< Tbase > &gradient, const Vector< Tbase > &diagonal_hessian, Tbase shift=0.1) const
memberdef.name: precondition_search_direction

briefdescription.para: Formulate the diagonal orbital Hessian.
Rotation matrices.
Determine maximum step size; doi:10.1016/j.sigpro.2009.03.015.
Rotate the orbitals through the given parameters.

memberdef.definition: OrbitalHistoryEntry< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::make_history_entry
memberdef.argsstring: (const DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase > &density_matrix, const FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > &fock) const
memberdef.name: make_history_entry

briefdescription.para: Make an orbital history entry.

memberdef.definition: OrbitalHistoryEntry< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::evaluate_rotation
memberdef.name: evaluate_rotation

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the energy with a given orbital rotation vector.
Level shift step.

detaileddescription.para: Build the rotation-step context from the current iterate. Returns false if there are no orbital-rotation DOFs.

Wrong-sign-safe preconditioned-SD direction at level shift sigma: d_alpha = -g_alpha / (sigma + max(0, h_alpha)). Uses the diagonal Hessian estimate h_alpha when it is positive (Newton-like step in the well-conditioned direction) and drops it otherwise (scaled SD in the ill-conditioned or wrong-sign direction). sigma >= initial_level_shift_ > 0 keeps the denominator strictly positive throughout.
Assemble the anti-Hermitian rotation generator K (one block per orbital block) from a flat amplitude vector d.
Maximum step length along exp(t K): one quasi-period of the largest-magnitude eigenvalue of -i K (which is real, since K is anti-Hermitian).

memberdef.type: std::pair< DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase >, FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > >
memberdef.definition: std::pair< DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase >, FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::evaluate_rotation_at_
memberdef.argsstring: (const Orbitals< Torb > &K, Tbase t, const RotationStepContext &ctx)
memberdef.name: evaluate_rotation_at_

detaileddescription.para: Evaluate the Fock builder at the rotated iterate C_pseudo * exp(t K) with the same occupations as the reference iterate. Increments the global Fock-evaluation counter.

memberdef.argsstring: (const RotationStepContext &ctx, const Orbitals< Torb > &C_trial, const FockMatrix< Torb > &F_trial) const
memberdef.name: directional_gradient_at_trial_

detaileddescription.para: Directional gradient at a trial iterate, computed from F^MO at that iterate: g_trial_a = 2 Re[(C_trial^dag F_trial C_trial)_{i,j}](n_j - n_i). F^MO is computed once per block and reused across DOFs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const RotationStepContext &ctx, Trial0DirectionFunc trial_0_direction, Vector< Tbase > &d_accepted, const char *tag)
memberdef.name: sigma_line_search_

detaileddescription.para: Line search along the unitary curve C(t) = C_pseudo * exp(t K) at fixed occupations. trial_0_direction(sigma) returns the initial descent direction at sigma = initial_level_shift_, including whatever curvature correction the caller applies (PR+ CG, L-BFGS, ...).

Two nested loops:
Inner t-walk: at fixed (direction, K, t_max) start at t = min(1, t_max) and refine by cubic-Hermite minimisation using the directional gradient at every rejected trial. Each step uses (E_ref, slope at t=0) and (E_trial, slope at t_trial) to locate the next interior minimum of the cubic; iterates until either a descent step is found, t falls below 1e-6 * t_max, or max_t_trials evaluations have been spent. This captures the points where the directional gradient changes sign, both "overshot" (slope > 0 at t_trial) and "non-monotonic" (slope < 0 still but the energy has risen and fallen).
Outer sigma fallback: if the t-walk exhausts trials, the direction itself is suspect; raise sigma (geometric or cubic-Hermite-in-sigma when first-trial slope data is on hand) and rebuild the preconditioned SD direction. Returns true and writes d_accepted on success.

Apply the PR+ CG mix in place to a preconditioned-SD direction when compatible state from the previous call is on hand. Falls through silently (leaves d unchanged) otherwise; rejects the CG direction if it has lost descent character.
Preconditioned PR+ scaled-steepest-descent step on the orbital rotations at fixed occupations.
Release the L-BFGS state, returning its storage to the heap. No-op when the user has not (yet) invoked the L-BFGS step at all, since the LBFGSState object is allocated lazily.

L-BFGS two-loop recursion applied to the current gradient ctx.g, using the wrong-sign-safe diagonal H_0 = diag(1/(sigma + max(0, h))) as the initial inverse-Hessian approximation. Returns the search direction d = -H_k g, where H_k is the limited-memory inverse Hessian built from the stored (s_i, y_i, rho_i) triples.

Apply the L-BFGS correction to d in place. d on entry is the preconditioned-SD direction (L-BFGS with empty history); replace it with the full two-loop direction when the stored history is compatible with the current DOFs and the resulting direction still descends. Otherwise leave d as preconditioned SD.

L-BFGS step on the orbital rotations. Mirrors scaled_steepest_descent_step but builds the trial-1 direction from a limited-memory BFGS approximation to the inverse Hessian rather than from PR+ CG. The LBFGSState struct is allocated lazily on the first call, so callers that never enable L-BFGS do not pay for the deque headers.

memberdef.definition: std::vector< FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::evaluate_batch_
memberdef.name: evaluate_batch_

detaileddescription.para: Evaluate the Fock builder on a batch of densities. Dispatches to batched_fock_builder_ when set, otherwise loops over fock_builder_. Either way, number_of_fock_evaluations_ is incremented once per density.

briefdescription.para: List of occupied orbitals.
List of occupied orbitals.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Orbitals< Torb > &C_ref, const FockMatrix< Torb > &F_ref, const OrbitalOccupations< Tbase > &n_ref, Orbitals< Torb > &C_pseudo, OrbitalEnergies< Tbase > &eps_out) const
memberdef.name: pseudo_canonicalise_

detaileddescription.para: True iff every occupation in the current iterate is within occupation_change_threshold_ of an integer (i.e. the iterate sits at a vertex of the skeleton polytope). Used by the SCF state machine to decide whether to hand control from ODA back to DIIS: fractional occupations need orbital relaxation through CG before DIIS can be trusted; integer occupations are stable targets that DIIS can extrapolate freely. Count degenerate orbital-energy clusters that lie inside the Aufbau-occupied window AND carry a non-trivial occupation difference, summed across blocks. Each such cluster contributes 1, regardless of how many orbitals or active rotation pairs it contains; this matches the preconditioned-CG convergence bound, which is the number of distinct eigenvalues of P^{-1} H, not the dimension of the bad subspace. With the wrong-sign-safe preconditioner all rotation pairs inside one near-degenerate cluster collapse to a single preconditioned eigenvalue (= sigma), so they need at most one CG iteration to relax as a group.

The Aufbau window is set, per block, by Aufbau-filling the current F^MO diagonals up to number_of_particles_ and taking the highest such orbital's energy plus a optimal_damping_degeneracy_threshold_ margin. Clusters sitting entirely above that edge cannot participate in the next occupation update and are dropped.

Clusters use the same ODA-style "cluster from start" definition as optimal_damping_step: walking orbitals in energy order, a cluster extends from its first orbital eps_start while eps_k - eps_start < optimal_damping_degeneracy_threshold_. A cluster contributes 1 iff its lowest energy sits at or below the window edge, it contains at least two orbitals, and the occupation spread inside it is at least occupation_change_threshold_. Pseudo-canonicalise the orbitals C_ref against the Fock F_ref, diagonalising F within each equal-occupation sub-block (defined by n_ref and occupation_change_threshold_). Rotating within an equal-occupation subset leaves the density invariant, so the pseudo orbitals still represent the same density and Fock; the diagonal entries of C_pseudo^dag F C_pseudo on the differently-occupied directions are canonical-orbital energy estimates and are returned in eps_out. Used by the rotation step, the active-rotation count, and the post-ODA burst-watch tripwires.

memberdef.argsstring: (const IndexVector &number_of_blocks_per_particle_type, const Vector< Tbase > &maximum_occupation, const Vector< Tbase > &number_of_particles, const FockBuilder< Torb, Tbase > &fock_builder, const std::vector< std::string > &block_descriptions)
memberdef.name: SCFSolver

briefdescription.para: Constructor.
Initialize the solver with a guess Fock matrix.
Initialize with precomputed orbitals and occupations.
Fix the number of occupied orbitals per block.
Get frozen occupations.
Set frozen occupations.
Get verbosity.
Set verbosity.
Get convergence threshold.
Set convergence threshold (unused if callback_convergence_function set).

detaileddescription.para: Register a batched Fock builder. When set, optimal_damping_step uses it for the axis-vertex sweep, sharing integral / grid setup across the N_par builds. The single-density fock_builder remains in use for mixed-density trials (model minimum, cubic edges, backoff scales). Passing a default-constructed std::function clears the override and restores the loop-over- fock_builder default.

briefdescription.para: Whether a batched Fock builder is registered.
Get energy gap below which orbitals are treated as degenerate in optimal damping.
Set energy gap below which orbitals are treated as degenerate in optimal damping.
Get the energy for the n:th entry.
Density matrix difference norm.
Get the used error norm.
Evaluate the norm.
Set the used error norm.
Get the maximum number of iterations.
Set the maximum number of iterations.

detaileddescription.para: Number of Fock-matrix builds performed by the most recent run. Reset to 0 inside run(); incremented every time a new density matrix is passed to the user-provided Fock builder.

briefdescription.para: When to start mixing in DIIS.
When to start mixing in DIIS.
When to switch over to DIIS.
When to switch over to DIIS.
Damping factor for DIIS diagonal.
Damping factor for DIIS diagonal.
DIIS restart criterion.
DIIS restart criterion.
Use optimal damping when max error bigger than this.
Use optimal damping when max error bigger than this.

detaileddescription.para: Number of orbital-rotation steps the run() state machine takes after each ODA step that transitions to CG. Zero (the default) means "use the polytope dimension of the last ODA call automatically".
Set the post-ODA orbital-rotation burst length. Zero restores the auto-from-polytope-dimension default.

briefdescription.para: Polytope dimension of the most recent ODA call (N_par).

detaileddescription.para: Number of orbital-rotation DOFs that lie inside a degenerate group at the iterate produced by the most recent ODA call. This is the default orbital-rotation burst length when orbital_rotation_steps_after_oda_ is left at 0; see compute_active_rotation_count().

briefdescription.para: Get maximum_history_length.
Set maximum_history_length.
Get oda_restart_steps.
Set oda_restart_steps.
Add entry to history, return value is True if energy was lowered.

memberdef.argsstring: (const DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase > &density, const FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > &fock)
memberdef.name: add_entry

briefdescription.para: Add entry to history, return value is True if energy was lowered.
Print the DIIS history.
Reset the DIIS history.

memberdef.definition: DiagonalizedFockMatrix< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::compute_orbitals
memberdef.name: compute_orbitals

briefdescription.para: Computes orbitals and orbital energies by diagonalizing the Fock matrix.
Determines the offset for the blocks of the iparticle:th particle.

memberdef.definition: std::vector< std::tuple< Tbase, size_t, size_t > > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::order_orbitals_by_energy
memberdef.name: order_orbitals_by_energy

detaileddescription.para: Collect orbital energies for a given particle type, sorted in increasing energy. Each tuple holds (energy, iblock, iorb).

briefdescription.para: Determine number of particles in each block.
Determines occupations based on the current orbital energies.
Check if we are converged.

detaileddescription.para: Run the SCF Run the SCF loop using the methods named in the input string.

The string is a +-separated, case-insensitive list of method tokens drawn from "DIIS" (Pulay's A/EDIIS-bracketed direct inversion in the iterative subspace), "ODA" (optimal-damping polytope step on the skeleton density matrices), and "CG" (preconditioned PR+ scaled steepest descent on orbital rotations at fixed occupations). Examples:

"DIIS" pure A/EDIIS extrapolation "ODA" standalone polytope minimisation "DIIS + ODA + CG" full compound algorithm (default) "ODA + CG" DIIS-less compound "DIIS + ODA + LBFGS" L-BFGS in place of PR+ CG (when both CG and LBFGS are listed L-BFGS is preferred)

State-transition rules: from DIIS we leave to ODA (or to CG when ODA is not allowed) on stall or large error; from ODA we hand to DIIS on integer occupations or to CG on fractional / failed occupations; from CG we burst orbital_rotation_steps_after_oda_ (or the polytope dimension when that is left at zero) steps and then hand back to DIIS. The state-machine collapses gracefully when only a subset of the methods is allowed: "DIIS" alone keeps retrying DIIS until maximum_iterations_ runs out; other subsets terminate early when every allowed method has failed in succession.

memberdef.definition: DensityMatrix< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::get_solution
memberdef.name: get_solution

briefdescription.para: Get the SCF solution.
Get the orbitals.
Get the orbital occupations.

memberdef.definition: FockBuilderReturn< Torb, Tbase > OpenOrbitalOptimizer::SCFSolver< Torb, Tbase >::get_fock_build
memberdef.name: get_fock_build

briefdescription.para: Get the Fock matrix builder return.
Get the Fock matrix for the ihist:th entry.
Finds the lowest "Aufbau" configuration by moving particles between symmetries by brute force search.
SCF solver class.
